mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly following UNRATE breaches above 5.5% in walk-forward tests (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand shock that outpaces supply adjustments, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, real consumer credit growth becomes the primary driver of equity cash flows; accelerating credit expansion lifts S&P 500 returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0% sustains corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% does not yield statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 reinforces the soft-landing narrative, reducing recession risk and boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surprises above 200k do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns in the majority of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while CPI remains sticky above 3.0%, real rates turn sharply positive, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint movement of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained periods, industrial production growth directly translates to pricing power and margin expansion, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 105.0 do not correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns when GDPC1 growth is below trend (IC < 0.03).
